item.title: I animated it
item.content:encoded:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=AO35iNssujQ

I hope you like it, Gibbon Guy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like the poem. I don't think that the interview that followed was necessary.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Pretty cool, though site 34 should be Site 34.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Memetic hazard alarms seem a bit goofy, but otherwise I like it. Poem was great, sort of bummed the grass prophets don't also speak in rhyme. I also thought the grass being a protector of sorts was an interesting concept, but a bit off stylistically. Still solid overall, +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I did this late because I forgot (sorry staff people).

The picture is my own, so creative commons.

I based the entire SCP off of the poem, which i wrote when I was bored one Sabbath (Saturday).

item.title: Site Criticism Team - Junior Staff Review
item.content:encoded:
Overall, you have the makings of an interesting article here, and this checks all of the boxes regarding basic competency regarding clinical tone and decent dialogue.

+ Open Crit
- Close Crit

That being said, there is one major area where this fails: the pacing. I found that the big reveals you included were wholly underwhelming. There's no real mystery or intrigue here, no budding curiosity in the reader or fridge logic to be found. You have 2 big reveals of sorts, and both of them fall flat because the reader never has the time nor the motivation to get invested in them beforehand.

The reveal that the grass will never stop growing unless 4740 is read, and that 4740-1 will one day refuse to read 4740 constitute reveal one, but both of these developments are basically summed up in throwaway lines tossed into the piece. This is the information that your piece was building up to ever since the con procs, and then it ends with you telling us flat out in both instances, without showing us what this means. This almost blindsides the reader with your intentions, rather than letting them figure it out on their own.

Your final twist falls flat as well, but for a different reason. It doesn't feel like a reveal at all. Oh, so 4740-1 can spit out memetic hazards (which are different from cognitohazards, btw), and these memes infect a whole wing? Well, first, that information came totally out of left field with no buildup whatsoever, and second, it doesn't pay off. The meme spreads, sure, but then it does nothing else. They're amnesticized and then its all over. That robs us of an exciting climax by giving us an unsatisfying shaggy dog style reveal.

Overall -1, but keep trying, because you have some of the harder aspects of SCP writing down for the most part.

Disclaimer: Opinions expressed in this review do not reflect the opinions of SCP Wiki staff as a whole. Please respond to TheMightyMcB or contact the captain of the Site Criticism Team, SoullessSingularity, for any questions or concerns relating to this review.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
this is pretty cool! i like it :3

+ nitpicks
- nitpicks
  • title should be "SCP-4740", not "Scp 4740"
  • you mention the containment chamber being trimmed a couple of times in the containment procedure, and i feel like for clarity that should be slightly adjusted to make it clear you mean "plant life within the chamber" and not the actual chamber? or alternatively clarity that the chamber is made of plant life?
  • "they are considered KIA. Any orders, or requests made by the individual should be ignored." is, weird phrasing; it's not technically wrong but it feels, weird to read?
  • "chakraphop Sisamouth" should have a capital C in Chakraphop
  • "But THE": the BUT should be capitalised for consistency
